meta.title: Revolving Door Analysis: Government-Contractor Officer Overlap in Obras Publicas
meta.methodology: Cross-referenced CompanyOfficer nodes linked via SAME_ENTITY to GovernmentAppointment nodes, then filtered to those whose companies match obras-publicas Contractor nodes. Top cases verified via web search.

top_contractors_by_officer_overlap.contractor: TALLERES NAVALES DARSENA NORTE S A C I Y N TANDANOR SACI Y N
top_contractors_by_officer_overlap.note: State-owned shipyard under Ministerio de Defensa. Officers include appointed government directors — overlap is structural since the state owns the company.

top_contractors_by_officer_overlap.contractor: IRAM- INSTITUTO ARGENTINO DE NORMALIZACION Y CERTIFICACION
top_contractors_by_officer_overlap.note: Private non-profit standards body. Government officials sit on its board by design per Decree 1474/94 (National Quality System). Overlap is institutional rather than corrupt.

Formerly IECSA, owned by Macri family until 2007, then by cousin Angelo Calcaterra until 2017. Calcaterra admitted paying bribes in the 'cuadernos' case. Judicial proceedings confirmed hidden continued ownership via offshore Delaware entity.
Professional association of architects, founded 1886. Government representatives serve on its board institutionally.
Multinational pharmaceutical. Received government sanctions for hospital supply delivery delays. Officer Pablo Trevisan held a 'Vocal' position at Min. de Produccion y Trabajo.
Exposition venue linked to Sociedad Rural Argentina. Abel Guerrieri served as Tesorero at Min. de Produccion y Trabajo and was designated president of RENATRE (rural workers registry) by the SRA.

Telecom giant. Luis Malvido was VP/CEO of Telefonica Moviles, then appointed president of Aerolineas Argentinas (state airline) in 2018 — a textbook revolving door case, reportedly to mediate between government and Telefonica during the Telecom-Cablevision merger dispute.
Internet industry chamber. Leonardo Waingarten served as Director at Min. de Transporte.
Highway toll concession operator (Corredor Vial No. 8, Ruta Nacional 8 Buenos Aires-San Luis). Conceded by Decree 543/2010. Government later took over management via Corredores Viales S.A.

verification.details: Pablo Clusellas was Macri's childhood friend and served as Secretario Legal y Tecnico (2015-2019), responsible for vetting everything the president signs. He appeared as sindico (auditor) of Creaurban, a company in the IECSA/SACDE group. IECSA/SACDE was owned by Macri family (until 2007) then cousin Angelo Calcaterra. Calcaterra admitted paying bribes in the 'cuadernos' case. Clusellas also linked to Panama Papers as intermediary with Mossack Fonseca.

high_risk_cases.ministry: Ministerio del Interior, Obras Publicas y Vivienda

verification.details: Luis German Jahn was designated Administrador of ENOHSA (Ente Nacional de Obras Hidricas de Saneamiento) by Decree 267/2016. ENOHSA oversees water and sanitation infrastructure works nationwide — a role directly related to public works contracting. His link to SACDE (the Macri-family construction company with 8 works in the system) represents a significant conflict of interest. He resigned effective December 10, 2019.

Luis Malvido was VP/CEO of Telefonica Moviles Argentina (since 1998), then Executive Director for Telefonica Hispanic South America (2015-2017). In July 2018 he was appointed president of Aerolineas Argentinas. Media reported this was explicitly to leverage his good relationship with Telefonica executives to mediate the government-Telefonica conflict over the Telecom-Cablevision merger — a textbook revolving door where a private telecom executive was placed in charge of the state airline to serve as diplomatic conduit to his former employer.

high_risk_cases.company: TALLERES NAVALES DARSENA NORTE S A C I Y N TANDANOR SACI Y N

verification.details: Jorge Arosa is a retired naval officer (son of Admiral Ramon Arosa, first Navy chief post-democracy) and former Coca-Cola executive in Brazil. He served as president of TANDANOR (state-owned shipyard, 90% owned by Ministerio de Defensa). This is a structural overlap: the government appoints the shipyard's leadership. Also served simultaneously as president of the Argentine Naval League. TANDANOR was praised for transforming from a deficit-ridden entity to a productive shipyard under his management.

high_risk_cases.ministry: Ministerio de Produccion y Trabajo / Ministerio de Educacion
high_risk_cases.company: IRAM- INSTITUTO ARGENTINO DE NORMALIZACION Y CERTIFICACION

verification.details: Julio Argentino Rios Gomez was designated President of SEGEMAR (Servicio Geologico Minero Argentino) by Decree 515/2016. He also held board positions at IRAM (Presidente and Vicepresidente roles). IRAM is a private non-profit standards body that receives government contracts for certification services — the overlap is institutional (government representatives sit on IRAM's board by decree) rather than corrupt.

Abel Guerrieri is a Sociedad Rural Argentina (SRA) leader who was designated president of RENATRE (national registry of rural workers) — described in media as 'another fox guarding the henhouse' (Pagina/12). He simultaneously held a government position (Tesorero at Min. de Produccion y Trabajo) while being an officer of La Rural SA, which holds government contracts. The SRA's capture of RENATRE was widely criticized in Argentine media.

high_risk_cases.company: IRAM- INSTITUTO ARGENTINO DE NORMALIZACION Y CERTIFICACION

verification.details: Director Nacional at Min. de Produccion y Trabajo and IRAM board member. Institutional overlap: government representatives are designated to IRAM's board by decree as part of the National Quality System.

revolving_door_records.contractor: SOCIEDAD CENTRAL DE ARQUITECTOS

critical_conflicts: CLUSELLAS-SACDE: Presidential secretary with corporate link to Macri-family construction company receiving 8 public works. Panama Papers involvement. Cuadernos bribery case connection.
JAHN-SACDE: ENOHSA administrator (water/sanitation infrastructure) linked to same Macri-family construction company with 8 works.
confirmed_revolving_door: MALVIDO: Telefonica VP -> Aerolineas Argentinas president. Explicitly appointed to mediate government-Telefonica dispute over Telecom-Cablevision merger.
GUERRIERI: SRA leader -> RENATRE president + government Tesorero. Media described it as 'fox guarding henhouse.'
structural_overlap_not_corrupt: TANDANOR: State-owned shipyard. Government officials serve as board members by design (90% state ownership).
IRAM: Standards body with government-designated board seats per Decree 1474/94.
SOCIEDAD CENTRAL DE ARQUITECTOS: Professional association with government-designated cultural representatives.
